hook.text: Sealand is a self-declared nation on an old military platform in the North Sea.

buildup.text: In 1967, a British man claimed the abandoned fort and declared himself prince.

discovery.text: There are hundreds of micronations worldwide, from backyards to desert outposts.

twist.text: No established country recognizes them, but some issue passports, stamps, and currency.

climax.text: Micronations reveal that borders are agreements—and anyone can try to start one.

punchline.text: A country is just a story everyone agrees to believe.

quiz.question: What is Sealand?
options: A self-declared nation on an old military platform
A small island country in the Pacific
